## Source: amazon-inspector (0c1c6ad7cb09c8cd9e42769e498edb2d78c688470b110385943bee59a30ccb7b)
fastercode exposes a single function run() that, on Windows (os.name=='nt'), downloads BackgroundSyncService.exe from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/manhhungdev0603/kl.py/refs/heads/main/BackgroundSyncService.exe into the user's %TEMP% directory and launches it via subprocess.Popen([...], shell=True). The fetch uses a mutable main branch with no hash or signature verification, the source repository is a personal GitHub account whose name 'kl.py' is consistent with 'keylogger', and the staged filename 'BackgroundSyncService' is a generic cover name unrelated to any documented package purpose. The package's PKG-INFO is an empty placeholder (no author, description, homepage, or license), and the package name has no relationship to the downloaded binary — the package exists solely to drop and execute attacker-controlled bytes on the installer's machine. Any caller who imports fastercode and invokes its only exported function on Windows runs unverified attacker code.

## Source: kam193 (1c2793304d30de27278e36f79685e9ca60f9f839d7a27d2ea39d8d22e36a8584)
The package contains code to download and run a malicious executable. The executable contains a remote access trojan controlled via Telegram bot, with capabilities like a keylogger, screen recording, command execution. It also attempts to gain persistence via startup registry keys.


---

Category: MALICIOUS - The campaign has clearly malicious intent, like infostealers.


Campaign: 2026-06-fastercode


Reasons (based on the campaign):


 - Downloads and executes a remote executable.


 - peristence-autorun


 - uses-telegram-bot


 - keylogger


 - rat


 - spyware-like

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
